seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with LlamaIndex + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with LlamaIndex + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Agentic RAG with LlamaIndex enables multi-step retrieval agents that autonomously plan queries, fetch from diverse sources, and synthesize attributed responses, powering advanced knowledge applications. However, this complexity introduces risks like irrelevant retrievals, hallucinations beyond sources, outdated indexes, and context loss from chunking. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure source faithfulness, planning accuracy, and consistency, safeguarding reliability in high-stakes deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with LlamaIndex
architectureOverview.description:
LlamaIndex's Agentic RAG uses a ReAct-style agent with tools for query decomposition, multi-index retrieval, and synthesis. The agent receives a user query, plans sub-queries via an LLM planner, executes retrieval from vector/index stores (e.g., VectorStoreIndex, SummaryIndex), and synthesizes using a response synthesizer with source attribution. Tools like QueryEngineTool enable modular retrieval from multiple data sources.

The flow involves: 1) Router/planner decomposes complex queries; 2) Parallel retrieval from hybrid indexes; 3) Synthesizer with citation tracking; 4) Self-reflection for multi-turn refinement. This autonomy boosts recall but amplifies failure modes like drifting from sources or inconsistent cross-retrieval synthesis.

Verification checkpoints span planning (sub-query relevance), retrieval (context pertinence), synthesis (grounding), and citations (traceability).

risks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
risks.description: Agent-planned sub-queries retrieve mismatched chunks, degrading synthesis quality
risks.mitigation: Verify retrieval relevance scores and semantic alignment with Vex before synthesis

risks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
risks.description: Agent LLM fabricates details not grounded in retrieved contexts during multi-hop reasoning
risks.mitigation: Enforce source faithfulness checks on final synthesis with citation traceability

risks.name: Index staleness causing outdated responses
risks.description: Agents serve expired knowledge from unrefreshed indexes without freshness validation
risks.mitigation: Timestamp verification and staleness detection during retrieval verification

risks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
risks.description: Semantic splits fracture multi-sentence concepts across chunks, losing agent reasoning continuity
risks.mitigation: Cross-chunk context continuity checks and metadata preservation verification

implementation.title: Multi-Index Agent Setup
implementation.description: Create ReAct agent with query engines for vector and summary indexes
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core.agent import ReActAgent
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SummaryIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader

documents = SimpleDirectoryReader("data").load_data()
vector_index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
summary_index = SummaryIndex.from_documents(documents)

vector_tool = QueryEngineTool.from_defaults(vector_index.as_query_engine())
summary_tool = QueryEngineTool.from_defaults(summary_index.as_query_engine())

agent = ReActAgent.from_tools([vector_tool, summary_tool], llm=llm, verbose=True)

implementation.title: Query Decomposition & Execution
implementation.description: Agent autonomously plans and executes multi-step retrieval
implementation.code:
response = agent.chat("Compare Q3 2024 revenue across all departments and forecast Q4 trends")
print(response)
# Agent internally: plans subqueries -> retrieves -> synthesizes with citations
